compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

New local shell/session/worktree writes on this machine are **attributed but unsigned**:
there is no persistent `[identity] signing_key`, no `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`, and no
`.rebar/current_identity` pointer. The store currently holds **0 signed events out of ~5097
in-scope events** (`rebar verify-identity --all` reports 0 `verified`) — which agrees with the
committed `rebar.toml` rollout-prep note "the store currently holds 0 signed events". (An
earlier draft said "~4 demo signed events"; the measured count is 0, so this Why is corrected
to match the codebase artifact.) Until every local session signs in steady state, flipping
`identity.require_authenticated` would reject every local write (bug `27ae-8322-9278-4a7c`).

## Precondition — identity already exists (verified here, NOT created)

The Rebar Bot **identity ticket already exists** in the store: `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`
("Rebar Bot"), `email = joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`, with **one registered ed25519 public
key** (`ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIHe3p+OG3L79IIh99qRBEkAEXaU+VbeQcSfhIu4M2F8b`).
This machine's `git config user.email` is `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`, so
`resolve_current_identity` resolves to 594c by the **git-email fallback**
(`src/rebar/_commands/identity.py:530-544` `resolve_current_identity`, which calls
`_match_by_email` at `:119-153`) — **no `.rebar/current_identity` pointer is required** (the
pointer only disambiguates when >1 identity shares an email; exactly one matches here). This story therefore **confirms/probes** the identity and its key; it creates
one only if the probe shows it absent. This machine operates *as* the bot (its git email is the
bot email), so it legitimately reuses the shared Rebar Bot key rather than minting a separate
per-clone identity.

## What

Provision a **persistent, machine-wide** signing key so every new shell/session/worktree on
this machine signs with no per-session setup:

- **Materialize** the Rebar Bot private key from AWS SSM SecureString
  `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` (ADR-0012) to a `0600` file **outside the repo** at
  `~/.ssh/rebar-bot-signing-key`. Never commit it; never echo it to logs.
- **Configure** `[identity] signing_key = "~/.ssh/rebar-bot-signing-key"` in the **XDG user
  config `~/.config/rebar/config.toml`** — the per-machine, git-ignored config the loader
  merges under `project` precedence (`config.py` `LAYER_ORDER = default,user,project,env,cli`;
  the project `rebar.toml` sets no `signing_key`, so the user value wins in **every** worktree).
  This file is the single chosen mechanism — preferred over exporting
  `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` from a shell profile because it is **worktree- and
  shell-profile-independent**: a fresh non-login shell in any worktree reads it with no
  re-export. (`.rebar/config.conf` is tombstoned — `src/rebar/_deprecations.py:193` — and must
  not be used; the shared, committed `rebar.toml` must not carry a per-machine key path.)
- **Confirm** the public key registered on identity 594c is the public half of the configured
  private key.

## Scope

This machine's local clones only. GitHub Actions signing (4dc0) and Gerrit-side signing (1a51)
are separate stories; the enforcement flip is 8d39. This story changes **no tracked repo
files** — it writes only `~/.config/rebar/config.toml` and `~/.ssh/rebar-bot-signing-key`, both
outside the repo (hence an empty `file_impact` is correct). The provisioning steps are captured
in the local runbook (AC5); a shared CLAUDE.md/ADR record of the pattern is deferred to the
enforcement-flip story.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Identity precondition confirmed.** `rebar show 594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d` is an `identity`
      with the bot email and ≥1 registered key. Proving command:
      `rebar show 594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d | jq -e '.ticket_type=="identity" and (.email|ascii_downcase=="joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com") and ((.keys|length)>=1)'`
      exits 0.
- [ ] **Registered public key equals the configured private key's public half.** Proving
      command:
      `diff <(ssh-keygen -y -f ~/.ssh/rebar-bot-signing-key | awk '{print $1,$2}') <(rebar show 594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d | jq -r '.keys[0]' | awk '{print $1,$2}')`
      exits 0.
- [ ] **git email matches the identity email.** Proving command:
      `test "$(git config user.email)" = "joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com"`.
- [ ] **A fresh worktree signs with no per-session setup.** From a worktree/shell *other* than
      where the key was configured, create a fresh probe ticket
      `t=$(rebar create task "signing-probe" | tail -1)`, then
      `rebar show "$t" | jq -e '.authorship.signed >= 1'` exits 0 (the write carries a
      signature). The event **verifies**, proven by its **absence** from the non-verified
      report (verified events are excluded from the report — `verify_authorship.py:503-504`
      `continue`): for a fresh, all-signed ticket,
      `rebar verify-identity --all --format json | jq -e '[.[]|select(.ticket_id==$t and .verdict!="verified")]|length==0' --arg t "$t"`
      exits 0 (zero non-verified entries for the probe ticket ⇒ its events verified).
- [ ] [operator-attested] The private key file is mode `0600`, lives outside the repo at
      `~/.ssh/rebar-bot-signing-key`, and the setup is captured in the local runbook. Evidence
      recorded on this ticket as a comment: the `ls -l ~/.ssh/rebar-bot-signing-key` mode line
      (proving `0600`), the SSM source param (`/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`), and the
      identity id (`594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`).
compiled_state.title: Provision persistent local write-signing on this machine (all worktrees/sessions)
